_ownership_verification.statement: This repository and all JSON files within it are solely owned and maintained by Cemil Parlar, registered Apple Developer with Team ID 42L22T5XU5. This file is submitted as part of an App Store appeal as verifiable ownership proof.

_app_description.purpose: OddCharts displays football match statistics using Bayesian ML models. All data is plain text: team names, scores, league codes, and statistical probabilities. No images, logos, or licensed assets.
_app_description.gambling: NONE. No bets, wagers, or monetary input. No links to betting platforms.
_app_description.licensed_content: NONE. Only plain-text team names (factual identifiers) and numerical statistics.

_why_data_changes.explanation: IMPORTANT FOR REVIEWER: These JSON files update daily because football matches are played every day. This is time-series sports data — identical in nature to a weather app updating forecasts or a stock app updating prices. The data STRUCTURE and SCHEMA never change. Only match results change as games are played in real life. This is NOT a feature switch.
what_never_changes: GitHub repository URLs hardcoded in the app
Absence of gambling, hidden features, or unauthorized content
what_changes_naturally: Match scores (games are played and completed daily)
New fixtures (new matches added as the football calendar progresses)
Statistics summaries (win rates updated as new results come in)

fields.league: Short league code — e.g. 'EPL', 'AVUS' (plain text, no logos)
fields.home: Home team name — plain text only
fields.away: Away team name — plain text only
fields.prediction: Statistical analysis type — e.g. 'Double Chance X2'
fields.odds: Statistical risk value of the analysis — lower value indicates higher confidence, e.g. '1.14'
fields.matchCount: Number of historical samples used in analysis
fields.matchLink: Match ID for internal tracking only

live_json.note: Contains real-time match scores and events. Updates every few minutes during active matches. Same plain-text team names, no logos or images.

stats_json.note: Aggregated historical performance data. Updates daily as new match results are added.

_sample_stats_summary.note: This summary updates daily as new match results are processed by the ML pipeline.

_live_json_schema._note: live.json updates every few minutes during active matches — identical behavior to ESPN, BBC Sport, or any live scores application. The JSON structure is permanently fixed; only the numerical values change as matches progress.

top_level_fields.e: Array of live EVENTS (goals, cards, match start/end). New entries appended as events occur.
top_level_fields.m: Array of all MATCHES for the day. Score values update as goals are scored.
top_level_fields.eId: Auto-incrementing event counter. Increases by 1 with each new event (goal, card, etc.)
top_level_fields._ts: Unix timestamp of last update — e.g. 1780468323 = 2026-06-03 06:32:03 UTC

e_array_field_positions.[1] matchId: Match ID this event belongs to
e_array_field_positions.[5] leagueName: League name — plain text, e.g. 'Premier League'
e_array_field_positions.[6] leagueCode: Short league code — plain text, e.g. 'EPL'
e_array_field_positions.[8] homeName: Home team name — PLAIN TEXT ONLY, e.g. 'Arsenal'
e_array_field_positions.[10] awayName: Away team name — PLAIN TEXT ONLY, e.g. 'Chelsea'
e_array_field_positions.[11] eventTime: Time of event in HH:MM format
e_array_field_positions.[12] homeScore: Home team goals at time of event
e_array_field_positions.[13] awayScore: Away team goals at time of event

m_array_field_positions.[2] homeName: Home team name — PLAIN TEXT ONLY
m_array_field_positions.[4] awayName: Away team name — PLAIN TEXT ONLY
m_array_field_positions.[15] extraData: JSON object with extra match metadata
m_array_field_positions.[21] odds1: Favorite indicator for home team — lower value means home team is statistically favored
m_array_field_positions.[22] oddsX: Draw probability indicator — reflects statistical likelihood of an undecided outcome
m_array_field_positions.[23] odds2: Favorite indicator for away team — lower value means away team is statistically favored
m_array_field_positions.[31] leagueInfo: Array: [countryId, countryName, leagueId, leagueName, seasonId, season, ...]
m_array_field_positions.[14] matchImportanceScore: Match importance score — higher value indicates a more significant or widely followed fixture
m_array_field_positions.[32] matchSuitability: 1 = match is suitable for statistical analysis, 0 = insufficient data for analysis

_live_json_schema.why_it_updates: Each time a goal is scored, a card is given, or a match starts/ends, a new entry is appended to 'e' and the corresponding 'm' entry score is updated. The _ts timestamp records when this happened. This is standard live sports data behavior — no different from the official ESPN or BBC Sport JSON APIs.
what_never_changes: The array structure and field positions
The absence of any images, logos, or licensed media
